name: Galicia (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the diagonal band, recalling the sea and the Galician sky

description: A white field crossed by a blue diagonal band running from the upper hoist to the lower fly, with the coat of arms of Galicia centred on the field.
history: The plain blue-on-white diagonal originated in the 19th century as the maritime ensign of A Coruna and came to be used as a Galician national flag during the late 19th century; recognised by the 1981 Statute of Autonomy and regulated (with the addition of the coat of arms on the official version) by Law 5/1984.

features.symbolism: A white field crossed by a single blue diagonal band; the colours echo the maritime arms of the city of A Coruna.

features.role: coat of arms of Galicia (a gold chalice surmounted by a Host on a blue field, surrounded by seven crosses, with a royal crown above)
features.symbolism: The chalice and Host evoke the cult of the Holy Sacrament long associated with Galicia and the cathedral of Lugo; the seven crosses represent the seven historic provinces of the Kingdom of Galicia.
